---
title: "Hypoxia Peak Reduced Report"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(knitr)

report_file <- knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)
report_dir <- if (nzchar(report_file)) dirname(report_file) else file.path("dev", "hypoxia_peak_reduced")
out_dir <- file.path(report_dir, "output")
peak_drift_dir <- file.path(out_dir, "peak_drift")
project_root <- normalizePath(file.path(report_dir, "..", ".."), winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)

summary_tbl <- read.csv(
  file.path(out_dir, "sample_peak_summary.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

input_tbl <- read.csv(
  file.path(out_dir, "prepared_input_table.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

ablation_tbl <- read.csv(
  file.path(out_dir, "ablations", "ablation_comparison.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

extension_tbl <- read.csv(
  file.path(out_dir, "extensions", "extension_comparison.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

karyotype_lookup_tbl <- read.csv(
  file.path(out_dir, "karyotype_flow_lookup.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

flow_only_g0g1_tbl <- read.csv(
  file.path(out_dir, "extensions", "adjacent_8n_delta_dna", "nuts", "flow_only_distribution", "g0g1_ploidy_distribution_summary.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

flow_only_g0g1_density_tbl <- read.csv(
  file.path(out_dir, "extensions", "adjacent_8n_delta_dna", "nuts", "flow_only_distribution", "g0g1_ploidy_density_grid.csv"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

dna_vectors <- readRDS(
  file.path(project_root, "processed_data", "hypoxia-sum159", "filtered_dna_area_vectors.rds")
)$raw

peak_drift_event_tbl <- if (file.exists(file.path(peak_drift_dir, "event_window_peak_drift.csv"))) {
  read.csv(
    file.path(peak_drift_dir, "event_window_peak_drift.csv"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
    check.names = FALSE
  )
} else {
  NULL
}

peak_drift_time_tbl <- if (file.exists(file.path(peak_drift_dir, "time_window_peak_drift.csv"))) {
  read.csv(
    file.path(peak_drift_dir, "time_window_peak_drift.csv"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
    check.names = FALSE
  )
} else {
  NULL
}

peak_drift_config_tbl <- if (file.exists(file.path(peak_drift_dir, "peak_drift_config.csv"))) {
  read.csv(
    file.path(peak_drift_dir, "peak_drift_config.csv"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
    check.names = FALSE
  )
} else {
  NULL
}

sample_order_tbl <- summary_tbl %>%
  arrange(as.Date(latest_match_date), sample_name) %>%
  transmute(
    sample_name,
    latest_match_date,
    sample_label = sprintf(
      "%s\n%s",
      sub("^Sample_SUM159_", "", sample_name),
      latest_match_date
    )
  )

sample_levels <- sample_order_tbl$sample_name
sample_label_map <- setNames(sample_order_tbl$sample_label, sample_order_tbl$sample_name)
outlier_palette <- c("Primary outlier" = "#cb181d", "Not primary outlier" = "#2171b5")

comparison_tbl <- bind_rows(
  ablation_tbl %>% mutate(model_family = "Ablation"),
  extension_tbl %>% mutate(model_family = "Extension")
) %>%
  mutate(
    free_parameter_count = as.numeric(free_parameter_count),
    rmse_cen = as.numeric(rmse_cen),
    rmse_g1 = as.numeric(rmse_g1),
    ablation_name = factor(
      ablation_name,
      levels = c(
        "baseline",
        "no_date_effect",
        "no_x_ratio_effect",
        "rho_fixed_one",
        "latent_u",
        "delta_dna",
        "adjacent_8n",
        "adjacent_8n_delta_dna"
      )
    )
  )

karyotype_vs_flow_tbl <- karyotype_lookup_tbl %>%
  select(
    karyo_id,
    flow_sample_name,
    flow_condition,
    latest_match_date,
    mapping_note,
    mean_dna_ploidy_using_supplied_unidentified,
    mean_dna_ploidy_using_average_unidentified
  ) %>%
  inner_join(
    flow_only_g0g1_density_tbl %>%
      mutate(
        ploidy = as.numeric(ploidy),
        log_density = as.numeric(log_density)
      ) %>%
      group_by(sample_name) %>%
      mutate(density_weight = exp(log_density - max(log_density, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
      summarise(
        mean_g0g1_ploidy = sum(ploidy * density_weight, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(density_weight, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      inner_join(
        flow_only_g0g1_tbl %>%
          transmute(sample_name, assigned_state_map),
        by = "sample_name"
      ),
    by = c("flow_sample_name" = "sample_name")
  )

plot_model_rmse_vs_complexity <- function(comparison_tbl) {
  plot_tbl <- bind_rows(
    comparison_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        ablation_name,
        model_family,
        free_parameter_count,
        peak_type = "CEN peak",
        rmse = rmse_cen
      ),
    comparison_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        ablation_name,
        model_family,
        free_parameter_count,
        peak_type = "G0/G1 peak",
        rmse = rmse_g1
      )
  )

  ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x = free_parameter_count, y = rmse, color = ablation_name, shape = peak_type)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
    labs(
      title = "Model fit error versus free parameter count",
      subtitle = "Ablations and extensions are shown together; point shape separates the CEN and G0/G1 error targets.",
      x = "Free parameter count",
      y = "RMSE",
      color = "Model",
      shape = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

plot_karyotype_vs_flow_ploidy <- function(karyotype_vs_flow_tbl, karyotype_col = "mean_dna_ploidy_using_average_unidentified") {
  ggplot(
    karyotype_vs_flow_tbl,
    aes(x = .data[[karyotype_col]], y = mean_g0g1_ploidy, color = assigned_state_map)
  ) +
    geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_point(size = 2.6, alpha = 0.9) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = flow_condition), nudge_y = 0.03, size = 2.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
    coord_equal() +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("2N" = "#1b9e77", "4N" = "#d95f02")) +
    labs(
      title = "Karyotype mean ploidy versus flow-derived mean G0/G1 ploidy",
      subtitle = "Flow ploidy is the posterior mean over the saved G0/G1 ploidy density grid from the adjacent_8n_delta_dna flow-only run.",
      x = "Mean karyotype-derived DNA ploidy",
      y = "Mean flow-derived G0/G1 ploidy",
      color = "Assigned state"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

plot_burden_vs_peak <- function(data, y_var, y_label, title, x_var = "ratio_above_below", x_label = "Burden proxy: ratio above / below", shape_var = NULL, line_var = NULL) {
  plot_tbl <- data %>%
    transmute(
      latest_match_date,
      burden_proxy = as.numeric(.data[[x_var]]),
      peak_value = as.numeric(.data[[y_var]]),
      shape_group = if (is.null(shape_var)) "All samples" else .data[[shape_var]],
      line_group = if (is.null(line_var)) "All samples" else .data[[line_var]]
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(burden_proxy), is.finite(peak_value), burden_proxy > 0, peak_value > 0) %>%
    mutate(
      latest_match_date = factor(latest_match_date, levels = sort(unique(latest_match_date))),
      shape_group = factor(shape_group),
      line_group = factor(line_group)
    )

  plot_obj <- ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x = burden_proxy, y = peak_value)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = latest_match_date, shape = shape_group), size = 2.4, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_x_log10(labels = label_number()) +
    scale_y_log10(labels = label_number(big.mark = ",")) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = x_label,
      y = y_label,
      color = "Sample date",
      shape = if (is.null(shape_var)) NULL else "Baseline class",
      linetype = if (is.null(line_var)) NULL else "Baseline class"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )

  if (is.null(line_var)) {
    plot_obj +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7, color = "black") +
      guides(
        shape = if (is.null(shape_var)) "none" else guide_legend(order = 2),
        linetype = "none",
        color = guide_legend(order = 1)
      )
  } else {
    plot_obj +
      geom_smooth(
        aes(linetype = line_group, group = line_group),
        method = "lm",
        se = FALSE,
        linewidth = 0.7,
        color = "black"
      ) +
      guides(
        color = guide_legend(order = 1),
        shape = guide_legend(order = 2),
        linetype = guide_legend(order = 3)
      )
  }
}

build_date_scaled_peak_tbl <- function(data, y_var, x_var = "ratio_above_below", class_var = "assigned_state") {
  plot_tbl <- data %>%
    transmute(
      sample_name,
      condition,
      latest_match_date,
      burden_proxy = as.numeric(.data[[x_var]]),
      peak_value = as.numeric(.data[[y_var]]),
      class_group = .data[[class_var]]
    ) %>%
    filter(
      is.finite(burden_proxy),
      is.finite(peak_value),
      burden_proxy > 0,
      peak_value > 0
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      latest_match_date = factor(latest_match_date, levels = sort(unique(latest_match_date))),
      class_group = factor(class_group)
    )

  class_fit_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(levels(plot_tbl$class_group), function(class_name) {
    class_data <- plot_tbl %>% filter(class_group == class_name)
    fit <- lm(log(peak_value) ~ log(burden_proxy), data = class_data)
    tibble(
      class_group = factor(class_name, levels = levels(plot_tbl$class_group)),
      intercept = unname(coef(fit)[1]),
      slope = unname(coef(fit)[2])
    )
  }))

  scaled_tbl <- plot_tbl %>%
    left_join(class_fit_tbl, by = "class_group") %>%
    mutate(
      pred_log_peak = intercept + slope * log(burden_proxy),
      fitted_peak = exp(pred_log_peak)
    ) %>%
    group_by(latest_match_date) %>%
    mutate(
      date_log_scale = mean(log(peak_value) - pred_log_peak, na.rm = TRUE),
      date_scale_factor = exp(date_log_scale),
      scaled_peak_value = peak_value / date_scale_factor
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      class_anchor_ploidy = if_else(class_group == "4N", 4, 2),
      raw_class_mean_peak = ave(peak_value, class_group, FUN = mean),
      date_scaled_class_mean_peak = ave(scaled_peak_value, class_group, FUN = mean),
      normalized_ploidy_raw = class_anchor_ploidy * peak_value / raw_class_mean_peak,
      normalized_ploidy_date_scaled = class_anchor_ploidy * scaled_peak_value / date_scaled_class_mean_peak,
      normalized_ploidy_date_burden = class_anchor_ploidy * scaled_peak_value / fitted_peak
    ) %>%
    inner_join(sample_order_tbl %>% select(sample_name, sample_label), by = "sample_name") %>%
    mutate(sample_name = factor(sample_name, levels = sample_levels))

  line_tbl <- expand.grid(
    class_group = levels(plot_tbl$class_group),
    burden_proxy = seq(
      min(plot_tbl$burden_proxy),
      max(plot_tbl$burden_proxy),
      length.out = 200
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    mutate(class_group = factor(class_group, levels = levels(plot_tbl$class_group))) %>%
    left_join(class_fit_tbl, by = "class_group") %>%
    mutate(fitted_peak = exp(intercept + slope * log(burden_proxy)))

  list(
    scaled_tbl = scaled_tbl,
    line_tbl = line_tbl,
    class_fit_tbl = class_fit_tbl
  )
}

plot_burden_with_date_scaling <- function(data, y_var, y_label, title, x_var = "ratio_above_below", x_label = "Burden proxy: ratio above / below", class_var = "assigned_state") {
  plot_parts <- build_date_scaled_peak_tbl(data, y_var, x_var = x_var, class_var = class_var)
  scaled_tbl <- plot_parts$scaled_tbl
  line_tbl <- plot_parts$line_tbl

  ggplot(scaled_tbl, aes(x = burden_proxy, y = scaled_peak_value)) +
    geom_line(
      data = line_tbl,
      aes(y = fitted_peak, linetype = class_group),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.7
    ) +
    geom_point(aes(color = latest_match_date, shape = class_group), size = 2.4, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_x_log10(labels = label_number()) +
    scale_y_log10(labels = label_number(big.mark = ",")) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = "Each date group is rescaled by one multiplicative factor chosen to minimize squared log-error against the original class-specific regression lines.",
      x = x_label,
      y = y_label,
      color = "Sample date",
      shape = "Baseline class",
      linetype = "Baseline class"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

build_ploidy_position_long_tbl <- function(data, y_var, x_var = "ratio_above_below", class_var = "assigned_state") {
  scaled_tbl <- build_date_scaled_peak_tbl(data, y_var, x_var = x_var, class_var = class_var)$scaled_tbl

  bind_rows(
    scaled_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        sample_name,
        sample_label,
        latest_match_date,
        class_group,
        class_anchor_ploidy,
        panel = "A. Raw / class mean",
        normalized_ploidy = normalized_ploidy_raw
      ),
    scaled_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        sample_name,
        sample_label,
        latest_match_date,
        class_group,
        class_anchor_ploidy,
        panel = "B. Date-scaled / class mean",
        normalized_ploidy = normalized_ploidy_date_scaled
      ),
    scaled_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        sample_name,
        sample_label,
        latest_match_date,
        class_group,
        class_anchor_ploidy,
        panel = "C. Date + burden normalized",
        normalized_ploidy = normalized_ploidy_date_burden
      )
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      panel = factor(
        panel,
        levels = c(
          "A. Raw / class mean",
          "B. Date-scaled / class mean",
          "C. Date + burden normalized"
        )
      ),
      sample_id = factor(sample_name, levels = rev(sample_levels)),
      centered_ploidy = normalized_ploidy - class_anchor_ploidy
    )
}

plot_ploidy_position_panels <- function(data, y_var, title, x_var = "ratio_above_below", class_var = "assigned_state") {
  plot_tbl <- build_ploidy_position_long_tbl(data, y_var, x_var = x_var, class_var = class_var)

  ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x = normalized_ploidy, y = sample_id)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(2, 4), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_point(aes(color = latest_match_date, shape = class_group), size = 2.6, alpha = 0.9) +
    facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 3) +
    scale_y_discrete(labels = rev(unname(sample_label_map[as.character(sample_levels)]))) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 3.5, 4, 4.5)) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = "Panels compare raw class-mean normalization, date-only scaling, and date-plus-burden normalization. Dashed lines mark the 2N and 4N reference positions.",
      x = "Normalized ploidy position",
      y = NULL,
      color = "Sample date",
      shape = "Baseline class"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = "grey80")
    )
}

plot_ploidy_sd_panels <- function(data, y_var, title, x_var = "ratio_above_below", class_var = "assigned_state") {
  plot_tbl <- build_ploidy_position_long_tbl(data, y_var, x_var = x_var, class_var = class_var)

  panel_label_tbl <- plot_tbl %>%
    group_by(panel) %>%
    summarise(
      sd_2n = sd(centered_ploidy[class_group == "2N"], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_4n = sd(centered_ploidy[class_group == "4N"], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      panel_label = sprintf(
        "%s\n%s, %s",
        panel,
        sprintf("SD(2N)=%.3f", sd_2n),
        sprintf("SD(4N)=%.3f", sd_4n)
      )
    )

  panel_labeller <- setNames(panel_label_tbl$panel_label, panel_label_tbl$panel)

  ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x = class_group, y = centered_ploidy)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_violin(aes(fill = class_group), alpha = 0.18, color = NA, width = 0.9) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = latest_match_date), width = 0.12, height = 0, size = 2.1, alpha = 0.9) +
    facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 3, labeller = as_labeller(panel_labeller)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("2N" = "#1b9e77", "4N" = "#d95f02")) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = "Centered ploidy displacement from each class anchor (2N or 4N). Facet labels report within-class SD for each normalization scheme.",
      x = "Baseline class",
      y = "Centered normalized ploidy",
      color = "Sample date",
      fill = "Baseline class"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = "grey80")
    )
}

plot_tumor_peak_ratio_vs_cen <- function(summary_tbl, input_tbl, title) {
  plot_tbl <- input_tbl %>%
    select(sample_name, cen_peak, tumor_peak_1, tumor_peak_2) %>%
    inner_join(
      summary_tbl %>% select(sample_name, latest_match_date, assigned_state, is_primary_outlier),
      by = "sample_name"
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      sample_name,
      latest_match_date = factor(latest_match_date, levels = sort(unique(latest_match_date))),
      assigned_state = factor(assigned_state),
      is_primary_outlier,
      cen_peak = as.numeric(cen_peak),
      tumor_peak_ratio = as.numeric(tumor_peak_2) / as.numeric(tumor_peak_1)
    ) %>%
    filter(
      !is_primary_outlier,
      is.finite(cen_peak),
      is.finite(tumor_peak_ratio),
      cen_peak > 0,
      tumor_peak_ratio > 0
    )

  fit_stats_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(levels(plot_tbl$assigned_state), function(class_name) {
    class_tbl <- plot_tbl %>% filter(assigned_state == class_name)
    fit <- lm(tumor_peak_ratio ~ cen_peak, data = class_tbl)
    tibble(
      assigned_state = factor(class_name, levels = levels(plot_tbl$assigned_state)),
      slope = unname(coef(fit)["cen_peak"]),
      r2 = summary(fit)$r.squared
    )
  }))

  annotation_tbl <- fit_stats_tbl %>%
    mutate(
      x = min(plot_tbl$cen_peak) * 1.02,
      y = seq(max(plot_tbl$tumor_peak_ratio) * 0.98, max(plot_tbl$tumor_peak_ratio) * 0.84, length.out = n()),
      label = sprintf("%s: slope = %.4g, R^2 = %.3f", assigned_state, slope, r2)
    )

  ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x = cen_peak, y = tumor_peak_ratio)) +
    geom_smooth(aes(linetype = assigned_state, group = assigned_state), method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(color = latest_match_date, shape = assigned_state), size = 2.4, alpha = 0.9) +
    geom_label(
      data = annotation_tbl,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      hjust = 0,
      vjust = 1,
      size = 3,
      label.size = 0.2
    ) +
    scale_x_log10(labels = label_number(big.mark = ",")) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Observed CEN peak",
      y = "Observed tumor peak 2 / tumor peak 1",
      color = "Sample date",
      shape = "Baseline class",
      linetype = "Baseline class"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

plot_peak_ratio_by_sample <- function(summary_tbl, input_tbl) {
  ratio_tbl <- input_tbl %>%
    select(sample_name, tumor_peak_1, tumor_peak_2) %>%
    inner_join(summary_tbl %>% select(sample_name, is_primary_outlier), by = "sample_name") %>%
    filter(!is_primary_outlier) %>%
    inner_join(sample_order_tbl, by = "sample_name") %>%
    mutate(
      sample_name = factor(sample_name, levels = sample_levels),
      secondary_primary_ratio = tumor_peak_2 / tumor_peak_1
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(secondary_primary_ratio))

  ggplot(ratio_tbl, aes(x = sample_name, y = secondary_primary_ratio, fill = latest_match_date)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.72, color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.4) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = sample_label_map) +
    labs(
      title = "Secondary-to-primary peak ratio by sample",
      subtitle = "Primary outliers are excluded. Dashed line marks a ratio of 2.",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Observed secondary / primary peak ratio",
      fill = "Sample date"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

build_histogram_plot_data <- function(summary_tbl, input_tbl, dna_vectors) {
  hist_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(names(dna_vectors), function(sample_name) {
    tibble(
      sample_name = sample_name,
      dna_a = dna_vectors[[sample_name]]
    )
  })) %>%
    filter(is.finite(dna_a)) %>%
    inner_join(sample_order_tbl, by = "sample_name") %>%
    mutate(sample_name = factor(sample_name, levels = sample_levels))

  base_peak_tbl <- input_tbl %>%
    select(sample_name, cen_peak, tumor_peak_1, tumor_peak_2) %>%
    inner_join(summary_tbl %>% select(sample_name, is_primary_outlier), by = "sample_name") %>%
    inner_join(sample_order_tbl, by = "sample_name") %>%
    mutate(
      sample_name = factor(sample_name, levels = sample_levels),
      outlier_status = if_else(is_primary_outlier, "Primary outlier", "Not primary outlier")
    ) %>%
    select(sample_name, sample_label, outlier_status, cen_peak, tumor_peak_1, tumor_peak_2)

  peak_tbl <- bind_rows(
    base_peak_tbl %>%
      transmute(sample_name, sample_label, outlier_status, peak_type = "CEN peak", peak_location = cen_peak),
    base_peak_tbl %>%
      transmute(sample_name, sample_label, outlier_status, peak_type = "Tumor peak 1", peak_location = tumor_peak_1),
    base_peak_tbl %>%
      transmute(sample_name, sample_label, outlier_status, peak_type = "Tumor peak 2", peak_location = tumor_peak_2)
  ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(peak_location))

  list(hist_tbl = hist_tbl, peak_tbl = peak_tbl)
}

plot_sample_dna_histograms <- function(summary_tbl, input_tbl, dna_vectors) {
  plot_parts <- build_histogram_plot_data(summary_tbl, input_tbl, dna_vectors)
  hist_tbl <- plot_parts$hist_tbl
  peak_tbl <- plot_parts$peak_tbl
  binwidth <- max(1000, diff(range(hist_tbl$dna_a, na.rm = TRUE)) / 90)

  ggplot(hist_tbl, aes(x = dna_a)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = binwidth, fill = "grey82", color = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
    geom_vline(
      aes(xintercept = peak_location, color = outlier_status, linetype = peak_type),
      data = peak_tbl,
      linewidth = 0.5,
      alpha = 0.9,
      show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ sample_name, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4, labeller = as_labeller(sample_label_map)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = outlier_palette) +
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c(
        "CEN peak" = "dotted",
        "Tumor peak 1" = "solid",
        "Tumor peak 2" = "longdash"
      )
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
    labs(
      title = "Per-sample raw DNA-A histograms with peak annotations",
      subtitle = "Peak markers are colored by each sample's primary-outlier status from the peak summary table.",
      x = "Raw DNA-A signal",
      y = "Cell count",
      color = NULL,
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

reshape_peak_drift_long <- function(peak_drift_tbl) {
  if (is.null(peak_drift_tbl) || !nrow(peak_drift_tbl)) {
    return(tibble())
  }

  peak_drift_tbl <- peak_drift_tbl %>%
    mutate(sample_name = sub("\\.fcs$", "", sample_name))

  bind_rows(
    peak_drift_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        sample_name,
        latest_match_date,
        relative_day,
        window_mid_fraction,
        time_mid_s = if ("time_mid_s" %in% names(peak_drift_tbl)) time_mid_s else NA_real_,
        peak_type = "CEN peak",
        peak_location = cen_peak,
        overall_peak = overall_cen_peak
      ),
    peak_drift_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        sample_name,
        latest_match_date,
        relative_day,
        window_mid_fraction,
        time_mid_s = if ("time_mid_s" %in% names(peak_drift_tbl)) time_mid_s else NA_real_,
        peak_type = "Tumor peak 1",
        peak_location = tumor_peak_1,
        overall_peak = overall_tumor_peak_1
      ),
    peak_drift_tbl %>%
      transmute(
        sample_name,
        latest_match_date,
        relative_day,
        window_mid_fraction,
        time_mid_s = if ("time_mid_s" %in% names(peak_drift_tbl)) time_mid_s else NA_real_,
        peak_type = "Tumor peak 2",
        peak_location = tumor_peak_2,
        overall_peak = overall_tumor_peak_2
      )
  ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(peak_location)) %>%
    inner_join(sample_order_tbl, by = "sample_name") %>%
    mutate(sample_name = factor(sample_name, levels = sample_levels))
}

plot_peak_drift_progression <- function(peak_drift_tbl, x_var = c("window_mid_fraction", "time_mid_s"), title, x_label, subtitle = NULL) {
  x_var <- match.arg(x_var)
  plot_tbl <- reshape_peak_drift_long(peak_drift_tbl)
  if (!nrow(plot_tbl)) {
    return(
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 1, y = 1, label = "Peak drift output not available for this view.") +
        theme_void()
    )
  }

  ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = peak_location, color = peak_type, group = peak_type)) +
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = overall_peak, color = peak_type), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.9) +
    geom_point(size = 0.9, alpha = 0.85) +
    facet_wrap(~ sample_name, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4, labeller = as_labeller(sample_label_map)) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "CEN peak" = "#1b9e77",
        "Tumor peak 1" = "#d95f02",
        "Tumor peak 2" = "#7570b3"
      )
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
    scale_y_log10(
      breaks = c(5000, 10000, 20000, 40000, 80000),
      labels = label_number(big.mark = ",")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      x = x_label,
      y = "Estimated peak location",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}

plot_peak_drift_time_seconds <- function(peak_drift_tbl) {
  plot_tbl <- reshape_peak_drift_long(peak_drift_tbl)
  if (!nrow(plot_tbl) || all(!is.finite(plot_tbl$time_mid_s))) {
    return(
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 1, y = 1, label = "Time-window peak drift is not available.") +
        theme_void()
    )
  }

  ggplot(plot_tbl, aes(x = time_mid_s, y = peak_location, color = peak_type, group = peak_type)) +
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = overall_peak, color = peak_type), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.9) +
    geom_point(size = 0.9, alpha = 0.85) +
    facet_wrap(~ sample_name, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4, labeller = as_labeller(sample_label_map)) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "CEN peak" = "#1b9e77",
        "Tumor peak 1" = "#d95f02",
        "Tumor peak 2" = "#7570b3"
      )
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)) +
    scale_y_log10(
      breaks = c(5000, 10000, 20000, 40000, 80000),
      labels = label_number(big.mark = ",")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Peak drift by acquisition time",
      subtitle = "Dashed lines show the whole-run peak estimates. Solid trajectories come from overlapping time windows.",
      x = "Window midpoint time (seconds)",
      y = "Estimated peak location",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}
```

# Data

This report uses the current checkpoint outputs under `output/`, including the
baseline sample summary, ablation comparison, extension comparison, and the
karyotype lookup tables, plus the raw DNA-A vectors in
`processed_data/hypoxia-sum159/filtered_dna_area_vectors.rds`.

# Current Takeaway

The current checkpoint interpretation is narrower than the earlier
`phi = 1`-focused story.

The strongest ablation result is that the date effect is real and needed:
removing it causes a large degradation in lower-peak fit. By contrast, removing
the burden effect changes the fit much less, and fixing `rho = 1` is nearly
irrelevant at this checkpoint.

Among the extensions, a pure adjacent-state reinterpretation appears too
aggressive because it pulls some ambiguous O2 samples all the way onto the 4N
track. The combined `adjacent_8n_delta_dna` model remains the more defensible
version because it allows limited state reinterpretation while still tracking
the observed peak geometry more closely than the plain adjacent-state model.

# Model Comparison

```{r model-comparison-scatter, fig.width=9, fig.height=5.5}
plot_model_rmse_vs_complexity(comparison_tbl)
```

The comparison still points to the same main conclusion: dropping the date
effect is the clear failure mode for G0/G1 fit, while the combined
`adjacent_8n_delta_dna` extension improves CEN fit but pays for that flexibility
with a worse lower-peak RMSE than the baseline model.

# Baseline Counterfactual

The baseline fit still implies a date-dependent counterfactual shift in the
lower peak, so the saved baseline counterfactual diagnostic remains useful to
show explicitly in the report.

```{r baseline-phi-counterfactual, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(out_dir, "phi_counterfactual.png"))
```

# Karyotype Link

These matched karyotype summaries do not prove the flow-state interpretation,
but they do help distinguish samples that are merely difficult to fit from
samples whose ambiguous lower peak also coincides with elevated inferred DNA
ploidy.

```{r karyotype-vs-flow-ploidy, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=6}
plot_karyotype_vs_flow_ploidy(karyotype_vs_flow_tbl)
```

# Peak Histograms

```{r raw-dna-histograms-with-peaks, fig.width=14, fig.height=12}
plot_sample_dna_histograms(summary_tbl, input_tbl, dna_vectors)
```

# Burden Proxy Plots: Fluorescence Ratio

```{r observed-g1-vs-burden, fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5}
plot_burden_vs_peak(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  y_label = "Observed G0/G1 peak",
  title = "Observed G0/G1 peak vs burden proxy",
  x_var = "ratio_above_below",
  x_label = "Burden proxy: fluorescence ratio above / below",
  shape_var = "assigned_state",
  line_var = "assigned_state"
)
```

```{r observed-cen-vs-burden, fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5}
plot_burden_vs_peak(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "cen_peak",
  y_label = "Observed CEN peak",
  title = "Observed CEN peak vs burden proxy",
  x_var = "ratio_above_below",
  x_label = "Burden proxy: fluorescence ratio above / below",
  shape_var = "assigned_state"
)
```

```{r observed-g1-vs-burden-date-scaled, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=4.8}
plot_burden_with_date_scaling(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  y_label = "Date-scaled observed G0/G1 peak",
  title = "Observed G0/G1 peak vs burden proxy after per-date scaling",
  x_var = "ratio_above_below",
  x_label = "Burden proxy: fluorescence ratio above / below"
)
```

```{r ploidy-position-panels, fig.width=15, fig.height=8}
plot_ploidy_position_panels(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  title = "Per-sample normalized ploidy positions under three normalization schemes",
  x_var = "ratio_above_below"
)
```

```{r ploidy-sd-panels, fig.width=15, fig.height=5.5}
plot_ploidy_sd_panels(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  title = "Spread around class anchors under three normalization schemes",
  x_var = "ratio_above_below"
)
```

```{r tumor-peak-ratio-vs-cen, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=5}
plot_tumor_peak_ratio_vs_cen(
  summary_tbl,
  input_tbl,
  title = "Tumor peak ratio versus CEN peak"
)
```

The burden plots remain useful descriptively, but they are no longer the main
interpretive result of this checkpoint. On log-log axes the CEN relationship
looks closer to a single monotone trend, whereas the observed G0/G1 peak still
separates into distinct 2N and 4N regimes. The follow-up G0/G1 panel asks how
much of the date-level spread can be absorbed by a single multiplicative shift
per date group while preserving the already fitted class-specific burden
slopes. The two faceted ploidy panels then compare raw, date-only, and
date-plus-burden normalization on the same 2N/4N anchor scale and summarize
how much within-class spread remains under each scheme.

# Burden Proxy Plots: DNA-Singlet Count Ratio

```{r observed-g1-vs-count-burden, fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5}
plot_burden_vs_peak(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  y_label = "Observed G0/G1 peak",
  title = "Observed G0/G1 peak vs burden proxy",
  x_var = "count_ratio_above_below",
  x_label = "Burden proxy: DNA singlet count ratio above / below",
  shape_var = "assigned_state",
  line_var = "assigned_state"
)
```

```{r observed-cen-vs-count-burden, fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5}
plot_burden_vs_peak(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "cen_peak",
  y_label = "Observed CEN peak",
  title = "Observed CEN peak vs burden proxy",
  x_var = "count_ratio_above_below",
  x_label = "Burden proxy: DNA singlet count ratio above / below",
  shape_var = "assigned_state"
)
```

```{r observed-g1-vs-count-burden-date-scaled, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=4.8}
plot_burden_with_date_scaling(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  y_label = "Date-scaled observed G0/G1 peak",
  title = "Observed G0/G1 peak vs burden proxy after per-date scaling",
  x_var = "count_ratio_above_below",
  x_label = "Burden proxy: DNA singlet count ratio above / below"
)
```

```{r count-burden-ploidy-position-panels, fig.width=15, fig.height=8}
plot_ploidy_position_panels(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  title = "Per-sample normalized ploidy positions under three normalization schemes",
  x_var = "count_ratio_above_below"
)
```

```{r count-burden-ploidy-sd-panels, fig.width=15, fig.height=5.5}
plot_ploidy_sd_panels(
  summary_tbl,
  y_var = "observed_g1_peak",
  title = "Spread around class anchors under three normalization schemes",
  x_var = "count_ratio_above_below"
)
```

This second block keeps the same descriptive pipeline but swaps in the
above/below ratio of DNA-singlet objects. That makes it easier to judge which
parts of the visual structure are robust to the burden-proxy definition and
which depend on using fluorescence intensity rather than object counts.

# Appendix

## Within-Run Peak Drift

The checkpoint outputs now include a sliding-window re-estimation of the CEN
peak and the two above-threshold tumor peaks within each run. These diagnostics
are still useful for ruling out acquisition artifacts, but they are secondary to
the cross-sample model comparison.

```{r peak-drift-event-progression, fig.width=14, fig.height=12}
plot_peak_drift_progression(
  peak_drift_event_tbl,
  x_var = "window_mid_fraction",
  title = "Peak drift by within-run event progression",
  subtitle = "Dashed lines show the whole-run peak estimates. Solid trajectories come from overlapping 10,000-event windows stepped by 2,500 events.",
  x_label = "Window midpoint / total acquired events"
)
```

If a valid FCS `Time` channel is available, the same peak re-estimation can
also be viewed on true acquisition time windows.

```{r peak-drift-time-progression, fig.width=14, fig.height=12}
plot_peak_drift_progression(
  peak_drift_time_tbl,
  x_var = "window_mid_fraction",
  title = "Peak drift by within-run time progression",
  subtitle = "This panel appears only when the saved gates plus source FCS files recover a valid Time channel. The x-axis is normalized acquisition time.",
  x_label = "Window midpoint / total acquisition time"
)
```

```{r peak-drift-time-seconds, fig.width=14, fig.height=12}
plot_peak_drift_time_seconds(peak_drift_time_tbl)
```

## Peak Ratio

```{r peak-ratio-by-sample, fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
plot_peak_ratio_by_sample(summary_tbl, input_tbl)
```
